methods.description: This method involves comparing a representative genomic 16S rDNA sequence of L. nanhaiensis DSM 24252 T with the Greengenes database to determine the weighted relative frequencies of taxa and keywords. This is used to identify the most frequently occurring genera and assess the phylogenetic relationship of L. nanhaiensis with other species.

steps.description: A representative genomic 16S rDNA sequence of L. nanhaiensis DSM 24252 T was compared with the Greengenes database.
steps.searchable_summary: compare 16S rDNA sequence with Greengenes database
inputs: 16S rDNA sequence of L. nanhaiensis DSM 24252 T
outputs: list of most frequently occurring genera

steps.description: Determine the most frequently occurring genera and calculate the average identity and coverage within HSPs.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate average identity and coverage within HSPs

steps.description: Identify the highest-scoring environmental sequence and calculate its identity and HSP coverage.
Figure 1 shows the phylogenetic neighborhood of L. nanhaiensis in a tree based on 16S rRNA gene sequences.

methods.description: This method involves using BLAST to compare the 1,429 bp long 16S rRNA gene sequence against the NCBI database to find similarities with other strains and assess the need for reclassification of L. nanhaiensis.

steps.description: BLAST results against the NCBI database with the 1,429 bp long 16S rRNA gene sequence showed 97% similarity to L. methylohalidivorans strain MB2, Phaeobacter gallaeciensis DSM 17395 and P. gallaeciensis 2.10.
steps.searchable_summary: perform BLAST search against NCBI database

steps.description: Assess the need for reclassification of L. nanhaiensis based on BLAST results and phylogenetic analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: assess reclassification need based on BLAST results
